etymology.description: semantic 土(土) (tǔ, "earth") + phonetic 保(保) (bǎo) -> fort; castle
etymology.interpretation: 堡(bǎo) uses 土(土) (tǔ, "earth") to emphasize its connection to earthworks or constructions made of earth, such as a fort or castle. 保(保) (bǎo) provides the pronunciation. The character thus evokes the idea of a fortified structure built upon or within the earth.

examples.chinese: 那座城堡非常壮观(那座城堡非常壯觀)。
我们在山顶建了一个堡垒(我們在山頂建了一個堡壘)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen zài shāndǐng jiàn le yī gè bǎolěi.
examples.english: We built a fort on the mountaintop.

examples.chinese: 堡上的旗帜随风飘扬(堡上的旗幟隨風飄揚)。
examples.pinyin: Bǎo shàng de qízhì suí fēng piāoyáng.
examples.english: The flag on the fort is waving in the wind.
